arts.ttl: Manchester United: Ruben Amorim responds to Marcus Rashford's links with Barcelona
arts.sum: Catalan giants latest big club to be credited with an interest in wantaway England forward

arts.ttl: Man Utd make contact with Barcelona over injured star
arts.sum: Andreas Christensen has been sidelined since August because of an Achilles tendon issue - but Man Utd have still been linked with a transfer.

arts.ttl: Official: One player misses out again as Barcelona name squad to face Real Betis
arts.sum: La Liga outfit Barcelona have on Wednesday unveiled the club’s squad list for their upcoming meeting with Real Betis. And it features one altogether noteworthy absentee… Barcelona are of course…

arts.ttl: Real Madrid forward declares love for Barcelona: “I would’ve made more sacrifices for Barça”
arts.sum: Signed for $6 million by Real Madrid, Cassano arrived from Roma as a huge star but left after scoring just four goals and providing three assists in 29 games . Such was his fascination with Barcelona that Cassano recently admitted he would have made more sacrifices to lead a less distracted life, as is usually required in elite soccer, if he had joined the Blaugrana club in 2006 instead of Real Madrid.

arts.ttl: ‘We lacked commitment to the team’ – Real Madrid manager Carlo Ancelotti on Barcelona defeat
arts.sum: Real Madrid manager Carlo Ancelotti has admitted that his side did not commit to the plan they had against Barcelona in the Spanish Supercup final, suffering a chastening defeat. Los Blancos face Celta Vigo in the Copa del Rey on Thursday night, but the Galician side were barely mentioned in the aftermath of the Clasico. […] The post ‘We lacked commitment to the team’ – Real Madrid manager Carlo Ancelotti on Barcelona defeat appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Barcelona thrashing forces Real Madrid into drastic transfer re-think
arts.sum: The Real Madrid coaching staff consider it “necessary” to sign a right-back in the January transfer window. Which right-backs could Real Madrid sign in the January transfer window?

arts.ttl: From Spain: No to Juventus, Deco Convinces Araujo to Stay at Barcelona
arts.sum: Ronald Araujo has decided to remain at Barcelona after a crucial meeting with the club's sporting director, Deco. This decision comes as a significant blow to Juventus, who had been actively pursuing the player to bolster their defensive lineup. The post From Spain: No to Juventus, Deco Convinces Araujo to Stay at Barcelona appeared first on FootItalia.com.

The FC Barcelona president appears in a press conference after winning the Spanish Super Cup to explain the registration process for Dani Olmo and Pau Víctor. After the superlative victory over Real Madrid in the Spanish Super Cup there has been practically no time to rest, with a midweek game at home to Betis in the Copa del Rey (Wednesday 9pm CET).

arts.ttl: Barcelona open to two sales in January window following transfer U-turn
arts.sum: Barcelona will swivel their transfer strategy this January transfer window, following the showdown talks between Sporting Director Deco and centre-back Ronald Araujo. With the Uruguayan now believed to be closer to staying at Barcelona, it will have consequences elsewhere in the squad. According to Sport, both Eric Garcia and Andreas Christensen will be made available […] The post Barcelona open to two sales in January window following transfer U-turn appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Araujo to stay at Barca? Saga taks fresh twist after Deco talks
arts.sum: Fabrizio Romano reports Araujo will NOT move this winter following a fresh round of talks with Deco and the club. Ronald Araujo may be set to stay at Barcelona after all after the saga regarding his future took a fresh turn on Wednesday.
